The proposed `repo.claim(id, {field:'steps.$[i].compensation.status', from:'pending', to:'done'}, ...)` cannot work: (a) `$[i]` is an arrayFilters positional identifier that is ILLEGAL in a query-filter key, and claim puts `field` into BOTH the filter (`[stateField]: fromSpec`, Repository.ts:1511) and the $set; (b) claim NEVER forwards arrayFilters — its internal updateActions.findOneAndUpdate call (Repository.ts:1637) passes only returnDocument/upsert/session, so even a valid `$[i]` update would not bind. The idiomatic streamline shape is a NUMERIC index path `steps.${i}.compensation.status` via the existing repo.updateOne (run.repository.ts:200) with explicit `from`-in-filter — exactly how the executor already claims steps (executor.ts:273-297). Use that, not claim() with array positionals. Secondary error: the cited `from===to` write-skip fast-path (Repository.ts:1622-1635) does NOT fire on the compensation-memoization path — the per-element claim uses `from:'pending'`, so an already-done element is a CAS MISS (returns null), never a from===to no-op; the perf justification is misattributed. claimVersion/incrementIfBelow/leasePlugin correctly identified as not needed here. batchTransaction suggestion for same-cluster exactly-once is sound and correctly optional.
verdict.breakingVerdict: Mostly honest as 'minor-additive', with caveats. New StepState.compensation field, new optional StepConfig knobs, new events, saga.durable opt-in defaulting false — all additive. BUT: adding 'compensating'|'compensated'|'compensation_failed' to the RunStatus union is a TYPE-LEVEL breaking change for any downstream TS consumer doing an exhaustive switch over RunStatus (e.g. a `never`-checked status mapper) — those stop compiling under isolatedModules/strict. That must be called out with a migration note; 'minor-additive' undersells it. Also: isTerminalState (status.ts:156) currently returns true only for done/failed/cancelled — if compensation runs while status==='failed' is observable, external watchers/SSE already see 'failed' as terminal (the design's own concern #2). Changing when 'failed' is emitted vs the new statuses is an observable behavior change for existing consumers even with saga.durable=false unless the new statuses are strictly gated behind the flag. Confirm the partial-unique idempotency index (run.model.ts:191-200) excludes the 3 new statuses or idempotency-reuse semantics shift.
requiredFixes: Drop `attempt` from ctx.idempotencyKey. Make it `${runId}:${stepId}[:scope]` — stable across retries AND crash/resume. Optionally expose a separate ctx.attempt already present (StepContextImpl.attempt) if a caller genuinely wants per-attempt keying, but the default external-call key must be attempt-INVARIANT or it double-charges.

Replace the `claim(field:'steps.$[i]...')` design with numeric-index CAS via repo.updateOne: `{_id, status:'compensating', `steps.${i}.compensation.status`:'pending'} → $set `steps.${i}.compensation.status`:'done'`, written DB-first AFTER the handler resolves, with CANCELLED_GUARD + bypassTenant — mirroring executor.ts:273. Do NOT route per-element transitions through mongokit claim() (no arrayFilters support).

Move compensation INTO a state-machine-driven phase that runs while status==='compensating', and REORDER engine.ts:702-711 so cleanupEventListeners/hookRegistry.unregister fire only on compensated/compensation_failed/cancelled — never while compensation is still pending. Make the `failed→compensating` assertAndClaim the first durable action so the multi-worker entry race resolves to one winner before any list is built.

Give the compensation phase a real heartbeat: build the compensation ctx with an AbortController + run the handler inside the executeWithTimeout heartbeat interval (executor.ts:399-446), or raise the stale threshold for compensating runs. Otherwise the StaleRunSweeper will markStaleAsFailed mid-compensation.

Re-derive the reverse-ordered completed-step list from freshly-read persisted StepState on BOTH the inline and recovery entry paths (not the in-memory `run.steps` snapshot at engine.ts:732), and define explicit semantics for steps left in `waiting`/`running` and for goto-looped steps that ran their effect more than once.

Document the exactly-once boundary honestly: memoization is effective-once ONLY for same-cluster writes; external compensations are effective-once ONLY if the handler passes the (now-fixed, attempt-invariant) idempotency key to the provider. State the crash-after-external-call-before-checkpoint window explicitly.

Call out the RunStatus union extension as a potential type-level break (exhaustive switches) with a migration note, and confirm the partial-unique idempotency index (run.model.ts:191-200) treatment of the 3 new statuses.

Validate at define-time that onCompensate handlers do not call suspending primitives (ctx.wait/waitFor/sleep/goto) — the design states this but it must be enforced (throw in define.ts:599) since a WaitSignal thrown from compensation has no handler in the new phase and would wedge a half-parked compensating run.
